name: American Bird Conservancy
summary: Bird-conservation nonprofit that works across the Americas to conserve wild birds and habitats through science, partnerships, habitat restoration, and threat reduction. Source: abcbirds.org.

provenance.notes: Mission, careers, and contact pages reviewed on American Bird Conservancy's official site.

name: Defenders of Wildlife
summary: U.S. nonprofit that protects native animals and their habitats in North America through legal advocacy, on-the-ground conservation, and policy work focused on keystone, carnivore, and migratory species. Source: defenders.org.

name: National Audubon Society
summary: U.S. nonprofit that uses science, policy, and community engagement to protect birds, wildlife, and the habitats on which they depend, including working lands, coasts, and climate refugia. Source: audubon.org.

name: National Fish and Wildlife Foundation
summary: Congressional chartered nonprofit (NFWF) that works with the public and private sector to protect and restore fish, wildlife, plants, and habitats in the U.S. through grantmaking and public-private delivery. Source: nfwf.org.

name: National Wildlife Federation
summary: National conservation nonprofit that works across the U.S. to restore wildlife populations, protect habitat, and advance policy and community action through regional centers and state and territorial affiliates. Source: nwf.org.

provenance.notes: Official about and careers pages reviewed on NWF's site.

name: Rocky Mountain Elk Foundation
summary: Membership-based conservation nonprofit focused on conserving elk, other wildlife, habitat, and hunting heritage through habitat projects, access work, and advocacy. Source: rmef.org.

provenance.notes: Mission and careers information reviewed on RMEF's official site.

name: The Conservation Fund
summary: National nonprofit that protects land, supports sustainable economies, and works with communities and partners on habitat protection, working forests, and conservation finance. Source: conservationfund.org.

provenance.notes: Official about and careers pages reviewed on The Conservation Fund site.

name: The Nature Conservancy
summary: Global nonprofit that protects lands and waters through science, public-private partnerships, and easements, with a major U.S. program for habitat conservation, climate, and water security for people and nature. Source: nature.org.

name: Theodore Roosevelt Conservation Partnership
summary: Conservation advocacy nonprofit that unites hunting, fishing, and conservation partners to protect habitat, public lands, water, and access through policy and partnership work. Source: trcp.org.

provenance.notes: Mission, contact, and employment pages reviewed on TRCP's official site.

name: Wild Salmon Center
summary: Nonprofit focused on protecting wild salmon strongholds and the rivers, forests, wildlife, and communities that depend on them through science, campaigns, and durable habitat protections. Source: wildsalmoncenter.org.

provenance.notes: Mission and careers pages reviewed on Wild Salmon Center's official site.

name: Wildlife Conservation Society
summary: Conservation nonprofit that advances wildlife protection through science, field conservation, policy, and public engagement, with major U.S. and global programs on wildlife and wild places. Source: wcs.org.

provenance.notes: About and careers pages reviewed on WCS's official site.

name: American Fisheries Society
summary: Professional society that advances global fisheries, aquatic resource science, and NRM through journals, standards, and meetings; supports careers and public understanding of fish resources. Source: fisheries.org.

name: Atlantic States Marine Fisheries Commission
summary: Interstate compact commission that coordinates cooperative management, science, habitat work, and law-enforcement collaboration for Atlantic coastal fisheries shared across state lines. Source: asmfc.org.

provenance.notes: Mission and careers pages reviewed on ASMFC's official site.

name: Great Lakes Fishery Commission
summary: Binational commission established by treaty to coordinate Great Lakes fishery research, sea lamprey control, and cooperative management among U.S. and Canadian jurisdictions. Source: glfc.org.

provenance.notes: Home and about pages reviewed; the commission posts active hiring from its official site rather than a stable standalone careers page.

name: NOAA National Marine Fisheries Service (NOAA Fisheries)
summary: Federal agency (within NOAA) that stewards the nationâ€™s ocean fisheries, protected species, and marine habitats, conducts science, sets and implements management under the Magnuson-Stevens Act, and works with Councils, states, and international partners. Source: fisheries.noaa.gov.

provenance.notes: Headquarters in Silver Spring, MD area; also see regional offices. NOAA regions page: https://www.fisheries.noaa.gov/regions

name: NOAA Office of National Marine Sanctuaries
summary: NOAA program that manages the National Marine Sanctuary System, conserving nationally significant marine and Great Lakes waters through resource protection, research, education, and stewardship. Source: sanctuaries.noaa.gov.

provenance.notes: National Marine Sanctuary System headquarters and field-site jobs are posted via NOAA's official sanctuary pages.

name: Pacific States Marine Fisheries Commission
summary: Interstate fisheries commission serving Pacific states through fishery science, data systems, habitat and passage work, and cooperative management support for marine and anadromous species. Source: psmfc.org.

provenance.notes: Official working-with-us page reviewed on psmfc.org.

name: Trout Unlimited
summary: Non-profit dedicated to the conservation, protection, and restoration of North Americaâ€™s coldwater fisheries and their watersheds, through science, on-the-ground projects, and volunteer chapters. Source: tu.org.

name: Bureau of Land Management
summary: Federal land management agency (Interior) for more public surface acres than any other U.S. agency, managing rangeland, oil and gas, minerals, recreation, and wildlife habitat in multiple use under law and NEPA. Source: blm.gov.

name: Bureau of Reclamation
summary: Interior agency that manages, develops, and protects water and related resources in the western United States, including major responsibilities for reservoirs, water delivery, hydropower, environmental compliance, and fish and wildlife enhancement. Source: usbr.gov.

provenance.notes: Mission and hiring guidance reviewed on Bureau of Reclamation official pages.

name: Ducks Unlimited, Inc.
summary: Non-profit conservation organization dedicated to the conservation, restoration, and management of wetlands and waterfowl habitat in North America, with science-based delivery of habitat projects. Source: ducks.org.

provenance.notes: Memphis, TN; national and Canada programs (see international site for Canada career pages).

name: National Park Service
summary: Federal bureau (Interior) that preserves park units, cultural resources, and natural systems and provides visitor services, science, and resource stewardship in national parks and related sites. Source: nps.gov.

name: U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service
summary: Federal agency within the Department of the Interior that conserves, protects, and enhances fish, wildlife, plants, and their habitats, including the National Wildlife Refuge System, ESA, and international treaty programs. Source: fws.gov.

name: U.S. Forest Service
summary: Federal agency (USDA) that manages 193 million acres of national forests and grasslands, balancing timber, recreation, watersheds, and wildlife habitat, with a major role in public land stewardship and NRM. Source: fs.usda.gov.

provenance.notes: National HQ; forest regions nationwide.

name: USDA Natural Resources Conservation Service
summary: USDA's primary private-lands conservation agency that works with agricultural producers to protect soil, water, habitat, and working lands through technical assistance, conservation planning, and Farm Bill programs. Source: nrcs.usda.gov.

provenance.notes: Official mission and careers pages reviewed on nrcs.usda.gov.
